skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem:
x(kx − 72) = −24

In the given equation, k is an integer constant. If the equation has no real solution, what is the least possible value of k?
rationale: Distribute and rewrite in standard form: kx² − 72x + 24 = 0. The equation has no real solutions exactly when the discriminant is negative: (−72)² − 4(k)(24) < 0, i.e., 5184 − 96k < 0, so k > 54. The least integer satisfying this is 55.
